title: monarda citriodora subsp. austromontana
intro: Monarda citriodora subsp. austromontana is a subspecies of the Bee Balm plant, a perennial herb native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive citrus-scented flowers and leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and as an antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Monarda citriodora subsp. austromontana include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
